text: He frowned and turned ňĻhis attentionňļ away,
ňĻsimplyňļ because the blind man came to him ňĻinterruptingňļ.
You never know ňĻO Prophetňļ, perhaps he may be purified,
or he may be mindful, benefitting from the reminder.
As for the one who was indifferent,
you gave him your ňĻundividedňļ attention,
even though you are not to blame if he would not be purified.
But as for the one who came to you, eager ňĻto learnňļ,
being in awe ňĻof Allahňļ,
you were inattentive to him.
But no! This ňĻrevelationňļ is truly a reminder.
So let whoever wills be mindful of it.
It is ňĻwrittenňļ on pages held in honour‚ÄĒ
highly esteemed, purified‚ÄĒ
by the hands of angel-scribes,
honourable and virtuous.
Condemned are ňĻdisbelievingňļ humans! How ungrateful they are ňĻto Allahňļ!
From what substance did He create them?
He created them from a sperm-drop, and ordained their development.
Then He makes the way easy for them, <sup foot_note=1280>1280</sup>

foot_notes.1280: The way to true guidance or the way out of their mothers‚Äô wombs.

text: then causes them to die and be buried. <sup foot_note=1281>1281</sup>

foot_notes.1281: Burial is perceived as a way of honouring human beings after their death.

text: Then when He wills, He will resurrect them.
But no! They have failed to comply with what He ordered.
Let people then consider their food:
how We pour down rain in abundance
and meticulously split the earth open ňĻfor sproutsňļ,
causing grain to grow in it,
as well as grapes and greens,
and olives and palm trees,
and dense orchards,
and fruit and fodder‚ÄĒ
all as ňĻa means ofňļ sustenance for you and your animals.
Then, when the Deafening Blast <sup foot_note=1282>1282</sup> comes to pass‚ÄĒ

foot_notes.1282: This is the second Blast which will cause the dead to come to life for judgment. See 39:68.

text: on that Day every person will flee from their own siblings,
and ňĻevenňļ their mother and father,
and ňĻevenňļ their spouse and children.
For then everyone will have enough concern of their own.
On that Day ňĻsomeňļ faces will be bright,
laughing and rejoicing,
while ňĻotherňļ faces will be dusty,
cast in gloom‚ÄĒ
those are the disbelievers, the ňĻwickedňļ sinners.
